name: vtjson

metadata.name: vtjson
metadata.summary: A lightweight package for validating JSON like Python objects

documentation:
# vtjson

`vtjson` is an easy to use validation library compatible with Python type annotations.

## Introduction

Here is a simple schema:

```python
book_schema = {
    "title": str,
    "authors": [str, ...],
    "editor?": str,
    "year": int,
}
```

The following conventions were used:

- As in typescript, a (string) key ending in `?` represents an optional key. The corresponding schema (the item the key points to) will only be used for validation when the key is present in the object that should be validated. A key can also be made optional by wrapping it with `optional_key`.
- If in a list/tuple the last entry is `...` (ellipsis) it means that the next to last entry will be repeated zero or more times. In this way generic types can be created. For example the schema `[str, ...]` represents a list of strings.

Let's try to validate some book objects:

```python
good_book = {
    "title": "Gone with the Wind",
    "authors": ["Margaret Mitchell"],
    "year": 1936,
}

bad_book = {
    "title": "Gone with the Wind",
    "authors": ["Margaret Mitchell"],
    "year": "1936",
}

validate(book_schema, good_book, name="good_book")
validate(book_schema, bad_book, name="bad_book")
```

As expected `vtjson` throws an exception for the second object:

```text
Traceback (most recent call last):
          ...
    raise ValidationError(message)
vtjson.vtjson.ValidationError: bad_book['year'] (value:'1936') is not of type 'int'
```

We may also rewrite the `book_schema` as a valid Python type annotation.

```python
class book_schema(TypedDict):
    title: str
    authors: list[str]
    editor: NotRequired[str]
    year: int
```

Attempting to validate the bad book would raise the same exception as before.

Schemas can of course be more complicated and in particular they can be nested.
Here is an example that shows more of the features of `vtjson`.

```python
person_schema = {
    "name": regex("[a-zA-Z. ]*"),
    "email?": email,
    "website?": url,
}

book_schema = {
    "title": str,
    "authors": [person_schema, ...],
    "editor?": person_schema,
    "year": intersect(int, ge(1900)),
}
```

Let's try to validate an object not fitting the schema.

```python
bad_book = {
    "title": "Gone with the Wind",
    "authors": [{"name": "Margaret Mitchell", "email": "margaret@gmailcom"}],
    "year": "1936",
}
```

```text
Traceback (most recent call last):
          ...
    raise ValidationError(message)
vtjson.vtjson.ValidationError: bad_book['authors'][0]['email'] (value:'margaret@gmailcom') is not of type 'email': The part after the @-sign is not valid. It should have a period.
```

As before we can rewrite the new `book_schema` as a valid type annotation.

```python
class person_schema(TypedDict):
    name: Annotated[str, regex("[a-zA-Z. ]*")]
    email: NotRequired[Annotated[str, email]]
    website: NotRequired[Annotated[str, url]]

class book_schema(TypedDict):
    title: str
    authors: list[person_schema]
    editor: NotRequired[list[person_schema]]
    year: Annotated[int, ge(1900)]
```

For comprehensive documentation about `vtjson` see [https://www.cantate.be/vtjson](https://www.cantate.be/vtjson).

functions.name: vtjson.vtjson.safe_cast
documentation:
Validates the given object against the given schema and changes its type
accordingly.

```
param schema

the given schema

param obj

the object to be validated

return

the validated object with its type changed

raises ValidationError

exception thrown when the object does not
validate; the exception message contains an explanation about what went
wrong

raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is found
to contain an error
```

functions.name: vtjson.vtjson.make_type
documentation:
Transforms a schema into a genuine Python type.

```
param schema

the given schema

param name

sets the  attribute of the type; if it is not
supplied then  makes an educated guess

param strict

indicates whether or not the object being validated is
allowed to have keys/entries which are not in the schema

param debug

print feedback on the console if validation fails

param subs

a dictionary whose keys are labels and whose values are
substitution schemas for schemas with those labels

raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is found
to contain an error
```

functions.name: vtjson.vtjson.compile
documentation:
Compiles a schema. Internally invokes {py:func}`vtjson._compile`.

```
param schema

the schema that should be compiled

raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is found
to contain an error
```

functions.name: vtjson.vtjson._compile
documentation:
Compiles a schema.

```
param schema

the schema that should be compiled

param _deferred_compiles

an opaque data structure used for handling
recursive schemas; it should be passed unmodifed to any internal
invocations of  or


raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is found
to contain an error
```

functions.name: vtjson.vtjson.validate
documentation:
Validates the given object against the given schema.

```
param schema

the given schema

param obj

the object to be validated

param name

common name for the object to be validated; used in
non-validation messages

param strict

indicates whether or not the object being validated is
allowed to have keys/entries which are not in the schema

param subs

a dictionary whose keys are labels and whose values are
substitution schemas for schemas with those labels

raises ValidationError

exception thrown when the object does not
validate; the exception message contains an explanation about what went
wrong

raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is found
to contain an error
```

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.compiled_schema

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.compiled_schema.__validate__
documentation:
Validates the given object against the given schema.

```
param schema

the given schema

param obj

the object to be validated

param name

common name for the object to be validated; used in
non-validation messages

param strict

indicates whether or not the object being validated is
allowed to have keys/entries which are not in the schema

param subs

a dictionary whose keys are labels and whose values are
substitution schemas for schemas with those labels

return

an empty string if validation succeeds; otherwise an
explanation about what went wrong
```

The result of compiling a schema. A {py:class}`compiled_schema` is
produced by the factory function {py:func}`vtjson.compile`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.wrapper

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.wrapper.__compile__
documentation:
Compiles a schema.

```
param _deferred_compiles

an opaque data structure used for handling
recursive schemas; it should be passed unmodifed to any internal
invocations of  or


raises SchemaError

exception thrown when the schema definition is
found to contain an error
```

Base class for schemas that refer to other schemas.

Handling such schemas is somewhat delicate since `vtjson` allows them to
be recursive.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.comparable
documentation: Base class for objects that are comparable with each other.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.ValidationError
documentation:
Raised if validation fails. The associated message explains what went
wrong.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.SchemaError
documentation: Raised if a schema contains an error.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.Apply
documentation:
Modifies the treatement of the previous arguments in an `Annotated` schema.

```
param skip_first

if  do not use the first argument (the Python
type annotation) in an  construct for validation because this
is already covered by the other arguments

param name

apply the corresponding  command
to the previous arguments

param labels

apply the corresponding 
command  to the previous arguments
```

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.optional_key

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.optional_key.__init__
documentation:
---
param key: the key to be made optional
---

Make a key in a Mapping schema optional. In the common case that the key
  is a string, the same effect can be achieved by appending `?`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.union

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.union.__init__
documentation:
---
param schemas: collection of schemas, one of which should match
---

An object matches the schema `union(schema1, ..., schemaN)` if it matches
one of the schemas `schema1, ..., schemaN`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.intersect

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.intersect.__init__
documentation:
---
param schemas: collection of schemas, all of which should match
---

An object matches the schema `intersect(schema1, ..., schemaN)` if it
matches all the schemas `schema1, ..., schemaN`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.complement

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.complement.__init__
documentation:
---
param schema: the schema that should not be matched
---

An object matches the schema `complement(schema)` if it does not match
`schema`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.lax

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.lax.__init__
documentation:
---
param schema: |-
  schema that should be validated against with
  `strict=False`
---

An object matches the schema `lax(schema)` if it matches `schema` when
validated with `strict=False`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.strict

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.strict.__init__
documentation:
---
param schema: |-
  schema that should be validated against with
  `strict=True`
---

An object matches the schema `strict(schema)` if it matches `schema` when
validated with `strict=True`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.set_label

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.set_label.__init__
documentation:
---
param debug: |-
  it `True` print a message on the console if the schema
  was changed via substitution
param labels: labels that will be attached to the schema
param schema: the schema that will be labeled
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

An object matches the schema `set_label(schema, label1, ..., labelN,
debug=False)` if it matches `schema`, unless the schema is replaced by a
different one via the `subs` argument to `validate`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.quote

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.quote.__init__
documentation:
---
param schema: the schema to be quoted
---

An object matches the schema `quote(schema)` if it is equal to `schema`.
For example the schema `str` matches strings but the schema `quote(str)`
matches the object `str`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.set_name

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.set_name.__init__
documentation:
---
param name: name for use in non-validation messages
param reason: |-
  indicates whether the original non-validation message
  should be suppressed
param schema: the original schema
---

An object matches the schema `set_name(schema, name, reason=False)` if it
matches `schema`, but the `name` argument will be used in non-validation
messages.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.regex

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.regex.__init__
documentation:
---
param flags: the flags argument used when invoking `re.compile`
param fullmatch: |-
  indicates whether or not the full string should be
  matched
param name: |-
  common name for the pattern that will be used in
  non-validation messages
param regex: the regular expression pattern
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This matches the strings which match the given pattern.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.glob

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.glob.__init__
documentation:
---
param name: |-
  common name for the pattern that will be used in
  non-validation messages
param pattern: the wild card pattern to match
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

Unix style filename matching. This is implemented using
`pathlib.PurePath().match()`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.magic

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.magic.__init__
documentation:
---
param mime_type: the mime type
param name: common name to refer to this mime type
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

Checks if a buffer (for example a string or a byte array) has the given
mime type. This is implemented using the `python-magic` package.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.div

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.div.__init__
documentation:
---
param divisor: the divisor
param name: |-
  common name (e.g. `even` or `odd`) that will be used in
  non-validation messages
param remainder: the remainer
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This matches the integers `x` such that `(x - remainder) % divisor` == 0.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.close_to

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.close_to.__init__
documentation:
---
param abs_tol: the maximal allowed absolute deviation
param rel_tol: the maximal allowed relative deviation
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

This matches the real numbers that are close to `x` in the sense of
`math.isclose`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.gt

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.gt.__init__
documentation:
---
param lb: the strict lower bound
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This checks if `object > lb`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.ge

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.ge.__init__
documentation:
---
param lb: the lower bound
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This checks if `object >= lb`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.lt

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.lt.__init__
documentation:
---
param ub: the strict upper bound
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This checks if `object < ub`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.le

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.le.__init__
documentation:
---
param ub: the upper bound
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This checks if `object <= ub`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.interval

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.interval.__init__
documentation:
---
param lb: lower bound; ... (ellipsis) means no lower bound
param strict_lb: if True use a strict lower bound
param strict_ub: if True use a strict upper bound
param ub: upper bound; ... (ellipsis) means no upper bound
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
This checks if `lb <= object <= ub`, provided the comparisons make sense.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.size

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.size.__init__
documentation:
---
param lb: the lower bound for the length
param ub: |-
  the upper bound for the length; ... (ellipsis) means that
  there is no upper bound; `None` means `lb==ub`
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

Matches the objects (which support `len()` such as strings or lists) whose
length is in the interval `[lb, ub]`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.number
documentation: A deprecated alias for `float`.
Some predefined schemas

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.email

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.email.__init__
documentation:
---
param kw: |-
  optional keyword arguments to be forwarded to
  `email_validator.validate_email`
---

Checks if the object is a valid email address. This uses the package
`email_validator`. The `email` schema accepts the same options as
`validate_email` in loc. cit.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.ip_address

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.ip_address.__init__
documentation:
---
param version: the version of the ip protocol
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---
Matches ip addresses of the specified version which can be 4, 6 or None.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.url
documentation: Matches valid urls.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.date_time

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.date_time.__init__
documentation:
---
param format: format string for `strftime`
---

Without argument this represents an ISO 8601 date-time. The `format`
argument represents a format string for `strftime`.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.date
documentation: Matches an ISO 8601 date.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.time
documentation: Matches an ISO 8601 time.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.nothing
documentation: Matches nothing.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.anything
documentation: Matchess anything.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.domain_name

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.domain_name.__init__
documentation:
---
param ascii_only: if `False` then allow IDNA domain names
param resolve: if `True` check if the domain names resolves
---
Checks if the object is a valid domain name.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.at_least_one_of

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.at_least_one_of.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: a collection of keys
---

This represents a dictionary with a least one key among a collection of
keys.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.at_most_one_of

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.at_most_one_of.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: a collection of keys
---

This represents an dictionary with at most one key among a collection of
keys.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.one_of

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.one_of.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: a collection of keys
---

This represents a dictionary with exactly one key among a collection of
keys.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.keys

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.keys.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: a collection of keys
---

This represents a dictionary containing all the keys in a collection of
keys

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.ifthen

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.ifthen.__init__
documentation:
---
param else_schema: the `else_schema`
param if_schema: the `if_schema`
param then_schema: the `then_schema`
---

If the object matches the `if_schema` then it should also match the
`then_schema`. If the object does not match the `if_schema` then it should
match the `else_schema`, if present.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.cond

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.cond.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: list of tuples pairing `if_schemas` with `then_schemas`
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

An object is successively validated against `if_schema1`, `if_schema2`,
... until a validation succeeds. When this happens the object should match
the corresponding `then_schema`. If no `if_schema` succeeds then the
object is considered to have been validated. If one sets `if_schemaN`
equal to `anything` then this serves as a catch all.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.fields

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.fields.__init__
documentation:
---
param d: a dictionary associating fields with schemas
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

Matches Python objects with attributes `field1, field2, ..., fieldN` whose
corresponding values should validate against `schema1, schema2, ...,
schemaN` respectively

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.filter

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.filter.__init__
documentation:
---
param filter: the filter to apply to the object
param filter_name: common name to refer to the filter
param schema: |-
  the schema used for validation once the filter has been
  applied
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema definition is
  found to contain an error
---

Applies `callable` to the object and validates the result with `schema`.
If the callable throws an exception then validation fails.

classes.name: vtjson.vtjson.protocol

methods.name: vtjson.vtjson.protocol.__init__
documentation:
---
param dict: if `True` parse the object as a `dict`
param schema: |-
  a type annotated class (or class like object such as
  Protocol or TypedDict) serving as prototype
raises SchemaError: |-
  exception thrown when the schema does not support
  type_hints
---

An object matches the schema `protocol(schema, dict=False)` if `schema` is
a class (or class like object) and its fields are annotated with schemas
which validate the corresponding fields in the object.
